summary: Palantir MCP is a lightweight open-source CLI wrapper that bootstraps and installs the proprietary @palantir/mcp package from secure Foundry environments. It does NOT contain the actual MCP server implementation; instead it handles authentication, preflight validation (Node.js version, network connectivity, token expiry), configures NPM to use Foundry's internal package registry, and spawns the real @palantir/mcp server as a child process. The tool uses Foundry's Multipass authentication service for token TTL checks and the Authoring API for interactive token refresh via browser-based OAuth flow. The actual Ontology-facing MCP tools (object types, actions, queries) are inside the closed-source @palantir/mcp package that gets downloaded at runtime from the Foundry artifact registry.

ontologyConcepts.evidence: The entire repository is an MCP (Model Context Protocol) bridge between AI assistants (Claude Code, Cursor, VS Code Copilot, Cline) and the Palantir Foundry Ontology. It sets up the stdio transport for MCP communication and spawns the proprietary @palantir/mcp server that exposes Ontology operations as MCP tools.

Authentication is handled via Foundry user tokens (FOUNDRY_TOKEN env var or --foundry-token flag). Token validation uses the Multipass API (multipass/api/token/ttl) for TTL checking and the Authoring API (code/api/security/token/retrieve) for interactive browser-based token refresh. ConjureError handling distinguishes between INVALID_JWT (unauthorized) and Authoring:InvalidTokenSignature (bad token signature). Bearer token auth is used for NPM registry access.

The NPM registry URL path reveals a Foundry artifacts repository (ri.artifacts.repository.discovered.foundry-mcp) used for distributing the MCP package. The token refresh flow uses the Foundry Code/Authoring service endpoint (/workspace/data-integration/code/gradle/auth) which is part of Foundry's Data Integration pipeline infrastructure. This indicates the MCP server is distributed as a Foundry artifact through the platform's artifact management system.

The HttpRequestContext interface defines the contract for API communication with Foundry services (apiUrl + token). The CliOptions interface defines the user-facing configuration contract (foundryToken + foundryApiUrl). The SpawnOptions interface defines the contract for launching the real MCP server process. These are internal interfaces for the wrapper, not Ontology interfaces.

apiSurface.name: MultipassApi.getTokenTimeToLiveInSeconds
apiSurface.description: GET multipass/api/token/ttl - Returns the remaining TTL in seconds for the current Foundry authentication token. Used to determine if the token needs to be refreshed before spawning the MCP server.

apiSurface.name: AuthoringApi.retrieveToken
apiSurface.description: POST code/api/security/token/retrieve - Exchanges a browser-auth secret for a fresh Foundry token. Part of the interactive token refresh flow where the user authorizes in-browser and the CLI polls for the resulting token.

apiSurface.name: checkPackageAvailability
apiSurface.description: GET {npmRegistry}/@palantir/mcp - Checks that the @palantir/mcp package is available in the Foundry NPM registry. Uses Bearer token authentication. The registry path is /artifacts/api/repositories/ri.artifacts.repository.discovered.foundry-mcp/contents/release/npm/.

apiSurface.name: parseArguments
apiSurface.description: CLI entry point using Commander.js. Accepts --foundry-api-url (required, URL to Foundry instance) and --foundry-token (required, from flag or FOUNDRY_TOKEN env var). Allows unknown options and excess arguments which get forwarded to the spawned @palantir/mcp process.

apiSurface.name: spawnMcp
apiSurface.description: Spawns 'npx -y @palantir/mcp@latest' as a child process with stdio inherited. Configures NPM_CONFIG_REGISTRY to point at Foundry's internal registry and sets the scoped auth token via NPM_CONFIG_//{host}{path}:_authToken environment variable. Forwards SIGINT/SIGTERM signals to the child.

apiSurface.name: TokenRefreshUtils.refreshTokenIfExpired
apiSurface.description: Orchestrates interactive token refresh: checks TTL via Multipass, generates a crypto random secret, opens browser to /workspace/data-integration/code/gradle/auth?secret={}&origin=palantir-mcp, then polls AuthoringApi every 1 second for up to 60 seconds until the user completes browser authorization.

architecturePatterns.description: This repo is a bootstrapper, not the actual MCP server. It validates prerequisites, authenticates, then spawns the real @palantir/mcp package from Foundry's private NPM registry. The actual MCP tools and Ontology integration are inside the closed-source @palantir/mcp package.

Uses Palantir's conjure-client library (FetchBridge) for typed HTTP API calls to Foundry services. The BaseApi class provides a generic GET/POST interface with JSON media types, user-agent tracking, and token-based authentication. API classes (MultipassApi, AuthoringApi) extend BaseApi with service-specific endpoints.

Sequential preflight checks before spawning: (1) Node.js version >= 18, (2) network connectivity to Foundry URL, (3) token validity/refresh, (4) package availability in registry. Each check has a dedicated error class with actionable user-facing messages.

When the Foundry token is expired (TTL < 300 seconds), the CLI generates a crypto secret, opens the user's browser to Foundry's auth page, then polls the Authoring API up to 60 times (1/sec) until the browser authorization completes and a new token is available.

Configures NPM to use Foundry as a private registry at /artifacts/api/repositories/ri.artifacts.repository.discovered.foundry-mcp/contents/release/npm/. Auth tokens are passed via the NPM_CONFIG scoped auth token environment variable pattern (NPM_CONFIG_//{host}{path}:_authToken).

The wrapper registers SIGINT and SIGTERM handlers that forward signals to the spawned child process, ensuring clean shutdown of the real MCP server when the AI assistant or user terminates the connection.

The project bundles to a single dist/index.js file via esbuild targeting Node 18 ESM, with @osdk/* and commander as external dependencies. This ensures the wrapper is lightweight and fast to download via npx.

typeDefinitions.name: CliOptions
typeDefinitions.definition: interface CliOptions { foundryToken: string; foundryApiUrl: URL }
typeDefinitions.purpose: Represents the parsed CLI configuration for connecting to a Foundry instance. The foundryApiUrl is the base URL of the Foundry deployment and the foundryToken is the user's authentication bearer token.

typeDefinitions.name: HttpRequestContext
typeDefinitions.definition: interface HttpRequestContext { apiUrl: URL; token: string }
typeDefinitions.purpose: Context object passed to all Foundry API clients (BaseApi subclasses). Encapsulates the Foundry API URL and authentication token needed for Conjure client RPC calls.

typeDefinitions.name: SpawnOptions
typeDefinitions.definition: interface SpawnOptions { npmRegistry: URL; foundryToken: string; args: string[] }
typeDefinitions.purpose: Configuration for spawning the real @palantir/mcp child process. Includes the Foundry NPM registry URL, validated authentication token, and the original CLI arguments to forward.

typeDefinitions.name: McpError
typeDefinitions.definition: class McpError extends Error { constructor(message: string, cause?: unknown) }
typeDefinitions.purpose: Base error class for all palantir-mcp wrapper errors. Provides a cause chain for structured error reporting.

typeDefinitions.name: NodeVersionError
typeDefinitions.purpose: Thrown when Node.js version is below 18. Provides actionable upgrade instructions.

typeDefinitions.name: NetworkError
typeDefinitions.purpose: Thrown when the Foundry API URL is unreachable. Suggests checking URL format, VPN/firewall, and browser access.

typeDefinitions.name: InvalidAuthTokenError
typeDefinitions.purpose: Thrown when the token is malformed (invalid JWT) or belongs to a different Foundry environment. Directs user to generate a new token at the correct Foundry workspace URL.

typeDefinitions.name: AuthenticationTimoutError
typeDefinitions.purpose: Thrown when the interactive browser-based token refresh flow exceeds the 60-second timeout without the user completing authorization.

typeDefinitions.name: PackageFetchError
typeDefinitions.purpose: Thrown when the @palantir/mcp package cannot be retrieved from the Foundry NPM registry. Directs user to contact Foundry support.

typeDefinitions.name: BaseApi
typeDefinitions.definition: class BaseApi { constructor(context: HttpRequestContext, baseApiPath: string, serviceName: string); get<T>(endpointPath, endpointName): Promise<T>; post<T>(endpointPath, endpointName, data): Promise<T> }
typeDefinitions.purpose: Generic HTTP API client built on Palantir's conjure-client FetchBridge. Provides typed GET/POST methods with JSON serialization, Bearer token auth, and user-agent reporting. Subclassed by MultipassApi and AuthoringApi.

typeDefinitions.name: MultipassApi
typeDefinitions.purpose: Client for Foundry's Multipass authentication service (multipass/api). Provides token TTL checking to determine if the user's auth token needs refreshing.

typeDefinitions.name: AuthoringApi
typeDefinitions.purpose: Client for Foundry's Code/Authoring service (code/api). Provides the token retrieval endpoint used during interactive browser-based OAuth token refresh.

typeDefinitions.name: TokenRefreshUtils
typeDefinitions.definition: class TokenRefreshUtils { constructor(apiUrl: URL, currentToken: string); refreshTokenIfExpired(): Promise<string | undefined> }
typeDefinitions.purpose: Orchestrates the interactive token refresh flow: checks token expiry via Multipass, opens browser for user authorization, and polls for the new token. Uses a minimum TTL threshold of 300 seconds.

dependencies.name: commander
dependencies.purpose: CLI argument parsing and validation. Handles --foundry-api-url, --foundry-token flags, environment variable fallback (FOUNDRY_TOKEN), and allows passthrough of unknown arguments to the spawned @palantir/mcp process.

dependencies.name: conjure-client
dependencies.purpose: Palantir's RPC client library. Provides FetchBridge for making typed HTTP calls to Foundry's Conjure-defined services (Multipass, Authoring), with built-in user-agent handling, JSON serialization, and ConjureError types for structured error handling.

dependencies.name: typescript
dependencies.purpose: TypeScript compiler targeting ES2022 with Node16 module resolution and strict mode enabled.

dependencies.name: vitest
dependencies.purpose: Test framework with V8 coverage provider. Tests use path aliases (@api, @auth) for import resolution.

dependencies.name: esbuild
dependencies.purpose: Bundles the project into a single dist/index.js ESM file targeting Node 18, with @osdk/* and commander as externals.

dependencies.name: @palantir/mcp
dependencies.purpose: The actual MCP server implementation, downloaded at runtime from Foundry's private NPM registry. This is the closed-source package that contains the real Ontology MCP tools. Not declared in package.json; fetched via npx from the Foundry artifact repository.
